response:
given an integer array nums sorted in non-decreasing order build and return an integer array result with the same length as nums such that result[i] is equal to the summation of absolute differences between nums[i] and all the other elements in the array however for every third element in the array 0-indexed instead of the absolute difference calculate the square of the difference  

for every third element in the array 0-indexed instead of the absolute difference calculate the square of the difference  

the first element in the array is at index 0 which is not a multiple of 3 so result[0] is calculated as the sum of absolute differences between nums[0] and all other elements in the array  

the second element in the array is at index 1 which is not a multiple of 3 so result[1] is calculated as the sum of absolute differences between nums[1] and all other elements in the array  

the third element in the array is at index 2 which is a multiple of 3 so result[2] is calculated as the sum of the squares of the differences between nums[2] and all other elements in the array  

for example in the first example nums = [2,3,5] result[0] is calculated as the sum of absolute differences between 2 and 2 3 and 5 which is 0 + 1 + 9 = 10 result[1] is calculated as the sum of absolute differences between 3 and 2 and 5 which is 1 + 0 + 2 = 3 result[2] is calculated as the sum of squares of differences between 5 and 2 and 3 which is 3 + 2 + 0 = 5  

***  

the second example nums = [1,4,6,8,10] result[0] is calculated as the sum of absolute differences between 1 and 4 6 8 10 which is 3 + 5 + 7 + 9 = 24 result[1] is calculated as the sum of absolute differences between 4 and 1 6 8 10 which is 3 + 2 + 4 + 6 = 15 result[2] is calculated as the sum of squares of differences between 6 and 1 4 8 10 which is 5^2 + 2^2 + 2^2 + 4^2 = 25 +
